items.text: SenderProvider struct and execute() method extracted from openai/mod.rs to openai/http.rs.
HTTP transport in http.rs is structurally symmetric with websocket.rs — each transport is a self-contained module.
HTTP transport produces identical ProviderEvent stream as before extraction (no behavioral change).
WebSocket connection established to wss://api.openai.com/v1/responses with rustls TLS.
WebSocket connection established to wss://chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses for OAuth auth.
WS upgrade handshake includes OpenAI-Beta header with version from named constant WS_BETA_VERSION.
Auth bearer token and chatgpt-account-id applied during WS upgrade handshake via AuthProvider::auth_headers.
permessage-deflate compression enabled on WS connection.
Ping/pong frames handled automatically by tokio-tungstenite.
Request payload wrapped in {"type": "response.create", ...} envelope before WS send.
WS Text messages parsed as JSON with type field extracted as event_type and fed through map_sse_event().
ProviderEvent stream from WS transport matches HTTP transport for identical request inputs.
WS error envelopes ({"type": "error", ...}) mapped to ProviderError with classified error types via classify_failed_error().
WsPool struct holds Vec<WsSlot>, pool config (min/max connections, idle timeout), cooldown state, and failure counter.
WsSlot struct holds WsState, established_at timestamp, and last_used timestamp.
WsState enum defined with four variants: Available(WsConnection), InUse, Disconnected, Disabled.
Pool protected by tokio::sync::Mutex. Lock held only for slot scan and state swap (microseconds), never for streaming duration.
Dynamic warm floor: target pool size is max(min_connections, active_count + headroom), capped at max_connections.
Pool proactively establishes connections to maintain headroom before callers need them.
Pool shrinks on idle: connections above the dynamic target closed after idle_timeout of inactivity.
Caller arriving when all slots InUse and pool at max_connections falls back to HTTP for that request.

WsPoolConfig holds min_connections (4), headroom (2), max_connections (16), idle_timeout (10 min), base_backoff (5s), max_backoff (5 min), reconnect_margin (5 min) — all configurable via ProviderConfig.
Caller finding Available takes connection and swaps slot to InUse.
Caller finding Disconnected swaps slot to InUse and attempts WS establishment.
InUse state guards against double-establishment on same slot: concurrent callers move to next slot.
After request: healthy connection returned as Available, broken connection marked Disconnected.
Connection reused across sequential requests on the same slot without re-establishing TLS.
When all recent establishment attempts fail, pool enters cooldown with exponential backoff.
During cooldown, all callers use HTTP. After cooldown expires, pool tries WS again on next request.
Successful establishment resets cooldown backoff to zero.
No caller is permanently stuck on HTTP unless WS establishment fails through full cooldown escalation to Disabled.
Callers that used HTTP due to pool-full rotate back to WS when a slot frees up on subsequent requests.
Circuit breaker: when backoff reaches max_backoff and final attempt fails, pool transitions to Disabled (irreversible within session).
Per-slot connection age tracked from establishment time via std::time::Instant.
Proactive reconnection triggered when connection age exceeds reconnect_margin before the 60-minute limit.
websocket_connection_limit_reached error triggers fresh connection and single request retry.
Connection dropped and recreated on any unexpected WS Close frame from server.
Warm floor established via parallel tokio::spawn on first pool access. First caller waits only for its own connection, remaining establish in background.
response.processed message sent on WS after receiving ProviderEvent::Done with response_id.
response.processed send failure logged at warn level and does not fail the request.
WS transport supports internal generate: false prewarm on fresh connections as a transport-level optimization.
Prewarm is best-effort — wasted without harm if another caller uses the connection before the real request.
No prewarm method on the Provider trait. Provider trait remains single-method (stream only).
WS transport attempted first for api.openai.com and chatgpt.com endpoints.
HTTP transport used for custom base_url endpoints without WS attempt.
HTTP fallback always available — pool-full, cooldown, Disabled, and establishment failure all degrade to HTTP.
Transport fallback logged at warn level with failure reason.
AuthProvider trait gains async auth_headers method returning Vec<(String, String)> of header name-value pairs.
OAuthAuthProvider implements auth_headers via AuthManager::auth().await, returning bearer token and chatgpt-account-id.
ApiKeyAuthProvider implements auth_headers returning bearer token only.
MockAuthProvider implements auth_headers consuming from the same token sequence as apply_auth.
DebugDumper gains write_ws_send method producing ws_send JSONL entry type for outgoing WS frames.
DebugDumper gains write_ws_lifecycle method producing ws_lifecycle JSONL entry type for connection events.
Incoming WS events logged via existing write_sse_event after SseEvent construction (same entry type as HTTP).
WS transport logs connection establishment, request start, event count, and completion at debug level.
Pool state changes (scale up, scale down, cooldown enter/exit, Disabled) logged at debug level.
tokio-tungstenite added as direct dependency in norn Cargo.toml with rustls-tls features.
No new external crates introduced beyond tokio-tungstenite (already in workspace patch).
